chunks.title: Hills & Sheep — Setup Changes
chunks.content:
## New Components Added

| Component | Quantity | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| New land tiles | 18 | Includes Hill tiles and Vineyard tiles — shuffle into main stack |
| Sheep tokens | 16 | Values: 1, 2, 3, or 4 sheep per token |
| Wolf tokens | 2 | Threaten shepherds' flocks |
| Shepherd meeples | 6 (1 per player color) | Distinct shape from normal followers |
| Cloth drawstring bag | 1 | Holds all 18 flock tokens |

## Setup Steps

- Shuffle all 18 new land tiles into the main tile stack.
- Place all 18 flock tokens (16 sheep + 2 wolves) into the cloth bag.
- Each player takes 1 Shepherd meeple in their color — added to personal supply alongside regular followers.

> **Note on Hill tiles (during play, not setup):** Whenever a Hill tile is drawn and placed, immediately draw a second tile face-down and slide it beneath the Hill tile. That hidden tile remains face-down all game, quietly shrinking the effective tile pool.

chunks.title: Hills & Sheep — New Components Quick Reference
chunks.content:
## Hill Tiles

- When you **draw a Hill tile**, immediately draw a 2nd tile face-down and place it **under** the Hill tile (stays hidden all game).
- You may deploy a follower on the Hill tile normally.

### Tie-Breaking with Hills

| Situation | Result |
|---|---|
| Feature scores and players are tied | Player with a follower on a Hill tile **within that feature** wins all points outright |
| Multiple tied players each have a Hill follower | Only those players share the points; others score 0 |
| No tied player has a Hill follower | Standard tie-sharing applies (all tied score full) |

---

## Shepherd Meeple

- Placed in a **field** instead of a normal follower. Obeys standard occupancy rules (cannot share with other shepherds or farmers).
- On placement: draw **1 token** from the bag immediately.

### Shepherd Decision — When Adjacent Field Extends

| Option | Action | Outcome |
|---|---|---|
| **(A) Score now** | Count 1 pt per sheep depicted on all accumulated tokens | Shepherd + tokens return to supply |
| **(B) Draw another token** | Pull 1 token from bag | Sheep → add to pile; Wolf → lose entire flock (shepherd unscored, all tokens back in bag) |

> **End of game:** Any shepherd still on the board scores **0 points**.

---

## Sheep & Wolf Tokens

| Token | Effect |
|---|---|
| Sheep (1 sheep) | +1 pt when scored |
| Sheep (2 sheep) | +2 pts when scored |
| Sheep (3 sheep) | +3 pts when scored |
| Sheep (4 sheep) | +4 pts when scored |
| Wolf (×2) | Shepherd loses entire flock immediately and returns unscored |

---

## Vineyard Tiles

- Some tiles show a vineyard (vine/grape illustration).
- When a Monastery is **completed** during the game (not end-game), each Vineyard tile in the 8 surrounding spaces grants **+3 bonus pts** to the monastery owner.

| Adjacent Vineyards | Bonus pts |
|---|---|
| 1 | +3 |
| 2 | +6 |
| 3+ | +3 per vineyard |

chunks.title: Hills & Sheep — Hill Tile: Mandatory Hidden Draw
chunks.content:
## Hill Tile — Step 1 Modification (Mandatory)

When the tile you draw is a **Hill tile**, Step 1 of your turn is modified:

| Sub-step | Action |
|---|---|
| 1a | Place the Hill tile face-up as normal |
| 1b | **Immediately draw a 2nd tile** and place it **face-down beneath the Hill tile** |

- This 2nd tile remains hidden for the entire game.
- This is **mandatory** — you cannot skip the face-down draw.
- Steps 2 and 3 proceed normally after both tiles are placed.

chunks.title: Hills & Sheep — Shepherd Scoring Choice
chunks.content:
## Shepherd Scoring Choice — When Your Field Expands

Whenever you place a tile that **extends a field where your shepherd already stands**, you must make a choice before normal follower placement (Step 2):

| Option | Action | Outcome |
|---|---|---|
| **A — Score now** | Shepherd scores immediately | +1 pt per sheep in flock; shepherd and all tokens return |
| **B — Expand flock** | Draw 1 more token from bag | Sheep: add to flock and continue; Wolf: flock destroyed, shepherd returns unscored |

> **End of game:** Any shepherd still on the board at game end scores **0 points** — you must score voluntarily before the last tile or lose the flock.

chunks.title: Hills & Sheep — New Scoring Sources
chunks.content:
## Hills & Sheep — Scoring Quick Reference

| Scoring Source | When Scored | Points |
|---|---|---|
| Shepherd / Flock (voluntary) | When player chooses to score their shepherd | 1 pt per sheep depicted on all accumulated flock tokens |
| Shepherd at end-game | End of game (shepherd still on board) | **0 pts** — must score before last tile |
| Vineyard bonus | When a Monastery is **completed** during the game | +3 pts per Vineyard tile among the 8 surrounding spaces |
| Vineyard (end-game monastery) | End-game scoring of incomplete monastery | **0 bonus** — vineyards only apply to completed monasteries |
| Hills tie-break | Whenever a tied feature is scored | Tied player(s) with a follower on a Hill tile score full points; non-Hill tied players score 0 |

### Key Details

**Shepherd/Flock:** Flock tokens show 1, 2, 3, or 4 sheep each. Score = sum of all sheep values across accumulated tokens. Tokens return to bag after scoring.

**Vineyard:** Count only Vineyard tiles in the 8 orthogonally/diagonally adjacent spaces. +3 pts each.

**Hills Tie-Breaking:** Does not add extra points — it decides *who* receives points in a tie. Applies to roads, cities, and farms.
